mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across 2005-2010 data, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing institutional investors to rotate out of equities and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below -0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, boosting aggregate demand and forcing equity valuations higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (logistic regression coefficient insignificant at p>0.10) over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates capacity constraints and rising input costs, triggering short-term profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or the mechanism's predictive accuracy drops below random chance (50%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases household income and consumer purchasing power, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across the full sample, or positive returns occur in fewer than 45% of high-growth payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth exceeds 2.5 percent, the Federal Reserve accelerates rate hikes to combat inflation, increasing discount rates and compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 58% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, reducing recession risk premiums and driving institutional capital allocation into equities for positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day return direction, or the combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile supply chains and weak business investment, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 65% of periods where INDPRO declines during recovery phases, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy falls below 48%.
